changes: OpenClaw converted yesterday’s beta into stable with v2026.4.14, published on 2026-04-14 13:03 UTC.
nanobot shipped v0.1.5.post1 on 2026-04-14 18:58 UTC, then updated its README/news stream with the release details.
PicoClaw posted another nightly build on 2026-04-15 01:02 UTC, keeping the edge-runtime lane visibly active between stable cuts.
SwarmClaw kept firing rapid orchestration releases, jumping to v1.5.49 on 2026-04-14 22:00 UTC after a same-day run through v1.5.47 and v1.5.48.

OpenClaw’s showcase/readme layer also exposed fresh evidence of broader use-case depth, including home automation from discovered controls, scheduled visual briefings, and accounting intake workflows.
whyItMatters: OpenClaw is still the pace-setter, and turning the beta into a same-cycle stable reinforces that the category anchor is shipping quickly, not just previewing work.
nanobot continuing to patch and widen channel/runtime behavior strengthens the lightweight challenger lane, not just its star-growth story.
PicoClaw’s nightly cadence keeps the cheap-hardware edge narrative credible even without a new stable tag.
SwarmClaw’s release velocity keeps the orchestration/control-plane branch hot, even if it remains much smaller than the anchor projects.
The new showcase evidence matters because it deepens the practical OpenClaw use-case map without changing the higher-level strategic thesis.
